details.text: Derivative: مَا “What or whatever” indicates generality and can encompass that which we are aware of as well as that which we may not be aware of. مَا is used when there is an element of unknowing involved, in contrast to الَّذِي (What, which) which is used for that which is known and identified.

Derivative: الْمَلِكِ (Al-Malik) means the Sovereign the King as opposed to مَالِك (Maalik) which means an owner. مَالِك (Maalik) is restrictive to that which is owned and used for smaller spheres but الْمَلِكِ by necessity encompasses a lot under his domain.

Derivative: الْقُدُّوسِ comes from قُدْس which means purity and used in respect to spiritual purity. It differs from تَزْكِيَة (Tazkiyah) or زَكَاة (Zakaat) because the latter refers to purity that is attained, whilst قُدْس refers to that which is inherently pure in of itself. الْقُدُّوسِ comes from the تَفْعِيل (Taf’eel) form of قَدَّسَ – تَقْدِيس (Taqdees). Taqdees is to declare or acknowledge that something is inherently pure. الْقُدُّوسِ is also from the forms of المُبالَغَة (greater emphasis and power) thus implying the extremely or incredibly pure.

Derivative: الْعَزِيزِ “The Ultimate Authority” combines two meanings: Respect and authority. It is in the فَعِيل form which means that it is an Ism Sifah (اِسْم صِفَة) which means that it is a constant, implying continuity and permanence.

Derivative: الْحَكِيمِ means “The Wise” or “Source of all wisdom”. It is in the فَعِيل form which means that it is an Ism Sifah (اِسْم صِفَة) which means that it is a constant, implying continuity and permanence.
- The verse ends mentioning four names of Allah which is unique and contrary to what is normal of ending with two names of Allah.

Derivative: انْفَضُّوا from اِنْفَضَّ - اِنْفِضاضًا which means to break off or disperse from something. اِنْفِضاض refers to something that breaks or breaks off and cannot be recovered. Here in respect to the Jumu’ah prayer انْفَضُّوا implies that they broke off and disrupted it in a manner that it could not be put back together again i.e., order could not be restored.

Derivative: الْأُمِّيِّينَ means to be illiterate, unlettered or the inability to read. It comes from أُمّ (Umm - Mother) implying that you are in the same state of education that you were when you came out from your mother.

Derivative: الْحِكْمَةَ (Al-Hikmah) – “Wisdom” refers to the practical dimension related to living by the Kitab. Al-Imam Malik and Al-Imam Ash-Shafi’i said that الْحِكْمَةَ (Al-Hikmah) means the Sunnah. The Sunnah reflects the practical teachings.

Allah states that He sent a Messenger among the people who could not read (الْأُمِّيِّينَ) and then states that the Messenger reads and recites upon them (يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ), thus manifesting the miracle of an illiterate person reading to them from a source, in the case where it is evident that usual speech differs from his reading.
Derivative: أَسْفَارًا refers to large volumes of books of knowledge.
Derivative: حُمِّلُوا (to be burdened) comes from حَمَّلَ – يُحَمِّلُ – تَحْمِيلًا and حُمِّلُوا is in the passive form. It means to be entrusted with a great responsibility.
Derivative: هَادُوا from هَادَ – يَهودُ means to be a Jew or to follow Judaism.
Derivative: مُلَاقِي from لَاقَى – يُلاقِي – مُلاقَاةً which means to meet i.e., bound to meet and catch up with someone or something.

Derivative: الْجُمُعَةِ (Al-Jumu’ah) from جَمَعَ – يَجْمَعُ - جَمْعًا which means to gather (together). The day of Al-Jumu’ah is literally the day of gathering. We will be gathered on the day of judgement on the day of Jumu’ah.
Derivative: اسْعَوْا the command plural form from سَعى which means to hurry, rush and be hasty to head or move towards something.
